mainEntity.name: Should my SaaS startup use Cloudflare or Vercel for edge computing?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on your architecture. Choose Cloudflare if you need: API-first edge computing, bundled DDoS/WAF security, multi-tenant support via Durable Objects, or predictable pricing at scale. Choose Vercel if you're building a Next.js frontend-heavy application with minimal backend logic. Many SaaS teams use both — Vercel for the marketing site, Cloudflare Workers for the API layer and product backend.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to migrate a SaaS product to Cloudflare Workers?

acceptedAnswer.text: A typical SaaS migration takes 60-90 days for production readiness. Phase 1 (setup + POC): 2-4 weeks. Phase 2 (DNS + canary migration): 3-4 weeks. Phase 3 (full rollout + optimization): 2-3 weeks. Using Clodo Framework can reduce this by 40-60% thanks to automated route generation, built-in canary deployment, and pre-configured monitoring. Small teams with a single API service have completed migrations in as little as 2 weeks.

mainEntity.name: What are the biggest risks of migrating to Cloudflare Workers?

acceptedAnswer.text: The top 3 risks are: (1) CPU time limits — Workers have a 10ms free / 30s paid CPU limit per request, which means compute-heavy operations must stay on origin. (2) Vendor lock-in — Durable Objects, KV, and R2 are Cloudflare-proprietary with no direct equivalents on other platforms. (3) Debugging complexity — distributed edge logs are harder to trace than centralized servers. Mitigation: use Clodo Framework's abstraction layer for portability, start with stateless endpoints, and set up Logpush from day 1.

mainEntity.name: How much does Cloudflare Workers cost for a SaaS startup?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cloudflare Workers is one of the most cost-effective edge platforms: the free tier includes 100,000 requests/day. The paid plan starts at $5/month and includes 10 million requests. At 100M monthly requests, expect $50-100/month — compared to $500-800/month for equivalent AWS Lambda@Edge usage. The biggest hidden savings come from zero egress fees, bundled security, and no per-region pricing multipliers.

mainEntity.name: Can I use Cloudflare Workers for a multi-tenant SaaS application?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — Cloudflare's Durable Objects are specifically designed for multi-tenant workloads. Each tenant gets an isolated Durable Object instance with its own storage, eliminating noisy-neighbor problems. Combined with Workers for request routing and KV for tenant configuration, you can build a fully isolated multi-tenant architecture at the edge.
